target.name: KoBold Metals
target.founder: Kurt House, Josh Goldman, Jeff Jurinak

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
KoBold Metals, a Series C climate and energy company that has raised over $1B to date, presents a valuation range informed by two primary methods applied against a peer universe of eight private companies and one public comparable (Linamar, LNZA). The peer set includes same-sector and adjacent-stage companies such as Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B Series C), Amogy ($700M Series C), and Redwood Materials ($6.0B Series D), with median valuation of $450M across the eight-company sample.

The stage-progression method, anchoring to KoBold's last post-money valuation of $2.0B and applying a typical Series C-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.7x, implies a forward valuation of $3.4B. The peer-median approach yields $450M, reflecting the broad distribution within the climate-tech sector (range: $120M–$6.0B). These methods triangulate to a median estimate of $1.9B, with a low-case floor of $382M and high-case ceiling of $3.9B.

The wide dispersion between peer-median and stage-progression reflects material uncertainty in the valuation landscape for early-stage climate infrastructure. Key uncertainties include stage-progression velocity in an uneven funding environment, technology de-risking timelines, and commodity price exposure. The current $2.0B+ valuation sits above peer median but below the stage-progression high case, consistent with KoBold's capital intensity and funding momentum relative to the peer cohort.
